text.title: Web Scraping Resilience Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every production scraper must satisfy plus run-the-checklist + skip-this-methodology. The resilience-config validator enforces them.

rule.statement: Inter-request delay per domain is a random value drawn from [1000ms, 3000ms] — not a fixed interval. Concurrent caps via p-limit / Semaphore (default 3 for unknown targets).
rule.rationale: Fixed intervals fingerprint the bot; commercial WAFs cluster on equal-spaced traffic.

rule.statement: Retry uses exponential backoff with jitter: `delay = base * 2^attempt + rand(0, 1000ms)`. Cap retries at 5; on the final attempt re-throw so the caller can log and skip.
rule.rationale: Exponential backoff gives the target recovery time; jitter prevents thundering-herd retries from concurrent workers.

rule.statement: Every page.$eval / locator() call is wrapped in safeExtract(fn, default) — catches timeout + missing-selector errors and returns the default. `waitForSelector` timeout MUST be ≤ 5000 ms.
rule.rationale: Unguarded extraction aborts the batch on the first absent element; the rest of the row would have been recoverable.

rule.statement: The scrape detects browser-crash errors ("Target closed", "Session closed", "Browser has been disconnected") and relaunches the browser, requeuing the failing URL. Crash count is recorded in the resilience config artefact.
rule.rationale: Browser crashes (renderer OOM, GPU reset) are routine in multi-hour scrapes; absent recovery, the run dies at the first crash.

rule.statement: Each session sets: realistic randomized User-Agent, `navigator.webdriver=undefined`, Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, Accept, plausible Referer. Use playwright-stealth (Playwright) / puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth (Puppeteer) for deeper fingerprint defence.
rule.rationale: Headless Chrome ships with navigator.webdriver=true as the first-pass detector signal; minimal overrides defeat naive detection.

rule.statement: Resilience settings live in a single config artefact (JSON) consumed by the scrape, not copy-pasted across runners. The artefact lists: delay range, max_retries, base_delay_ms, safe_extract_timeout, anti_detect_enabled, crash_recover_enabled, concurrency_cap.
rule.rationale: Settings drift across copy-pasted code is the dominant source of "we had jitter but the new runner didn't".

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (browser handle, target rate, anti-detect lib installed), execute: probe rate-limit → wire jitter → wire retry → wrap extracts → arm crash-recover → emit config artefact.
rule.rationale: Partial resilience (jitter but no retry; retry but no safeExtract) leaves a different failure mode dominant; all five layers compose.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) source has an SLA-backed public API — use it, (b) source has hardline anti-bot (Turnstile, frequent CAPTCHA) — a fundamental rethink is needed, not just resilience, (c) one-off scrape — manual retry is cheaper, (d) source explicitly forbids scraping and no legal exception.
rule.rationale: Resilience overhead is wasted where APIs exist or where the source actively rejects automation.
